title: rhynchosia viscidula
intro: Rhynchosia viscidula, also known as the sticky wild pea or sticky rhynchosia, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. Rhynchosia viscidula can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
